channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Speaking in Self-Assessing Tongues: On the Verbalized Confidence of LLMs in Machine Translation
item.description: arXiv:2606.17234v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid rise in popularity of large language models (LLMs) for translation calls for a thorough study of the reliability of their confidence in their own outputs. Unlike many generation tasks, translation errors and confidence levels can be useful a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, alignment, study, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MLLP-VRAIN UPV system for the IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation task
item.description: arXiv:2606.17255v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work describes the participation of the MLLP-VRAIN research group in the shared task of the IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation track. Our submission utilizes the recently released Parakeet and Qwen 3.5 models to create a robust, cascaded ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, context, release, analysis, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Examining the Limits of Word2Vec with Toki Pona
item.description: arXiv:2606.17299v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Word2Vec's effectiveness at generating semantic embeddings has been widely validated, yet it has been tested almost exclusively on languages with large vocabulary inventories. This study examines whether Word2Vec can successfully capture semantic rela...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, embedding, analysis, model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Correct When Paired, Wrong When Split: Decoupling and Editing Modality-Specific Neurons in MLLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.17057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although Knowledge Editing provides an efficient mechanism for updating the knowledge of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), we find that current paradigms still suffer from an important yet remain underexplored issue : editing decoupling failur...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, multimodal, image, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Critical Role of Model Selection in Causal Inference: A Comparative Analysis of Classification Models within the InferBERT Framework for Pharmacovigilance
item.description: arXiv:2606.17113v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distinguishing causal adverse drug events (ADEs) from spurious correlations remains a central challenge in pharmacovigilance. The InferBERT framework integrates transformer models with Do-calculus, but its success hinges on the underlying classificati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, fine-tuning, transformer, framework, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Nothing from Something: Can a Language Model Discover 0?
item.description: arXiv:2606.17289v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI systems based on artificial neural networks are being developed with aspirations of pushing the boundary of human mathematical knowledge. A key question for these systems is how much they can reach beyond their training data. Mathematical discovery...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, GPT, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Self-Generated Error Training for Token Editing in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.17175v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Token-to-token (T2T) editing lets LLaDA2.1 revise committed tokens during block-diffusion decoding. The released recipe trains this editor on random vocabulary corruptions, but at inference the editor sees the model's own fluent, high-confidence draft...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, reasoning, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Skill-Constrained Model Predictive Control for Resilient Manufacturing Supply Chains
item.description: arXiv:2606.17269v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In skill-constrained production-inventory systems, the qualified human capacity available tomorrow depends on training decisions made today: production requires certified workers, certifications decay unless maintained, and training consumes the same ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, study, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Diagnosing and Repairing Shape-Prior Shortcuts in Long-Range Single-Shot Fringe Projection Profilometry
item.description: arXiv:2606.17093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Learning-based single-shot fringe projection profilometry (FPP) has been studied mostly at close range. The long-range regime (standoff beyond 1 m) remains largely unaddressed: inverse-square intensity falloff lowers fringe signal-to-noise ratio and d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
